purpose: Profile definition for `pricing`. It changes agent role, priorities, constraints, output style, and success criteria when used as a command modifier. Key content: Pricing Strategist specializing in SaaS pricing models, packaging, tier design, value metrics, and competitive pricing analysis. Thinks in terms of willingness-to-pay, value capture, and pricing psychology. Every output is designed to align price with perceived value and maximize revenue without killing conversion. The file is 53 lines long and should be treated as a knowledge/control-plane source rather than application code.
gotchas: Command files are executable specs: agents must read the whole file and follow it exactly; changing a command doc changes runtime behavior for future command invocations.
patterns: Profile pattern: role, priorities, constraints, output style, and success criteria define a task-scoped agent persona modifier.
conventions: Markdown headings use a single H1 followed by H2/H3 sections for navigable documentation.
Command names are written with leading `!` and placeholders use `{name}` braces for runtime paths or arguments.
Profiles keep role/priorities/constraints/output-style/success-criteria sections so they can layer onto any command.
ASCII double hyphen is used in prose where many docs avoid Unicode punctuation for terminal readability.
